stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 project methodology options. The probability of favorable team experience and project size is uncertain, estimated between 33% and 45%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: Agile: $87K in good team experience and project size, $12K in bad team experience and project size. Waterfall: $74K in good team experience and project size, $44K in bad team experience and project size. Hybrid: $273K in good team experience and project size, $109K in bad team experience and project size. Kanban: $159K in good team experience and project size, $94K in bad team experience and project size. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.39: best option is Hybrid with EV=$173.0K. Hybrid is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [163.1, 182.8].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
Hybrid is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
Not performing minimax regret analysis to find the most robust choice
